content: Ad-hoc multi-agent orchestration pipelines that currently only automate static, pre-specified glue work can be reframed as a non-Markovian multi-objective M-MDP, where each agent’s explicit memory of past pipeline execution outcomes is stored in the shared M-MDP memory buffer to adaptively re-route workflows and resolve unforeseen data failures in real time. This allows the multi-agent pipeline to move beyond 80% partial automation toward full autonomous operation by learning from historical context instead of relying on hard-coded routing rules.
